Market Volatility (VIX) Signal
← Back to dashboard

Market Volatility (VIX) Signal

VIX market volatility signal: tracking investor fear and uncertainty.

As of 2035-10-01

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given the VIX level of 20.23, an MVS z-score of 0.91, and a 20-day momentum z-score of 1.35, this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Regime shift, not trend continuation (1).
  • The signal is currently in an ELEVATED regime, reflecting persistent implied volatility above historical medians (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Low.

Methodology Applied:

  • Regime mapping uses absolute thresholds (ELEVATED: 20 ≤ VIX < 30) and MVS z-scores (STRESSED: z ≥ +1.0) (1).
  • Component weighting prioritises the level z-score (40%) and percentile rank (25%) over short-term momentum (1).
  • Z-scores are calculated using robust median/MAD over rolling windows to handle outliers (1).
  • Market Volatility (VIX) Signal (Latest: 2026-02-19) (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the VIX level (20.23), which breached the 20-threshold on Feb 12 and has remained elevated (1).
  • Momentum shows a medium-term upward trend (d20_z: 1.35) despite a tactical five-day easing (d5_z: -0.18) (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A sustained move of the VIX index below 16 and MVS_z below 0.
  • Signal stability is deteriorating, with the frequency of transitions into the STRESSED regime increasing since early February (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Continued volatility persistence within the ELEVATED range as risk premiums adjust.
  • Upside risk: Transition to STRESSED/CRISIS triggered by a VIX spike above 30.
  • Downside risk: Reversion to NORMAL regime if VIX stays consistently below 20 for three days.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Tactical (weeks); VIX captures expected 30-day variance (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; serves as a critical thermometer for risk-off sentiment.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs risk sentiment and drawdown probability for cross-asset portfolios (1).
  • Rising MVS implies higher crash-risk compensation and a higher volatility risk premium, typically tightening risk appetite (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined.
  • Typically interacts with Credit Spreads; rising VIX with widening spreads confirms systemic stress (1).

Regime Context:

  • The current ELEVATED regime is newly persistent, following a period of CALM/NORMAL conditions in early January (1).
  • Direction of change: Strengthening (overall volatility levels are trending higher since inception of the February cluster).

Model Limitations:

  • VIX is a short-horizon gauge and not a directional timing tool (1).
  • Snapshot reporting may miss intraday or intramonth stress bursts (1).

Data & References:

  • Market Volatility (VIX) Signal (VIXCLS), observation date: 2026-02-19 (1).
  • Most influential: VIX level (20.23) and MVS z-score (0.91) (1).
  • CBOE SKEW Index and 21-day Realised Volatility would improve tail-risk and risk-premium assessments (1).

VIX index: market volatility and investor sentiment.
